perturbation.original_text: Owner will invoice such fees to Charterer on a monthly basis and Charterer will pay such invoiced amounts within 30 days of invoice date.
perturbation.changed_text: Owner will invoice such fees to Charterer on a monthly basis and Charterer will pay such invoiced amounts within 15 days of invoice date.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction by modifying the payment deadline from 30 days to 15 days. This contradicts with Exhibit A, section 1 of the agreement that specifies a payment term of 30 days, creating uncertainty regarding which payment term is legally binding.
perturbation.contradicted_text: All monthly Owner invoices to Charterer for rates and cost items will be paid by Charterer within 30 days of invoice date in accordance with Owner's normal payment protocols, which will be specified in the applicable invoice. Each monthly invoice shall be itemized to include charges by applicable vessel by day.
perturbation.justification: The changed text alters the payment deadline from 30 days to 15 days. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which states "All monthly Owner invoices to Charterer for rates and cost items will be paid by Charterer within 30 days of invoice date". This creates a clear inconsistency regarding the payment terms, making it an in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Owner agrees that said equipment shall be maintained in a seaworthy, staunch, tight and suitable condition and, to the best of Owner's knowledge, in compliance with all applicable laws and regulations.
perturbation.changed_text: Owner agrees that said equipment shall be maintained in a seaworthy and suitable condition. Owner is not responsible for compliance with any applicable laws and regulations.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction by removing the owner's responsibility for complying with applicable laws and regulations. This contradicts with Exhibit A, section 5 of the agreement that states that Owner shall comply in all material respects with applicable laws, including, without limitation applicable environmental, health, safety and financial responsibility laws, rules and regulations.
perturbation.contradicted_text: During the Term of this Agreement, Owner shall comply in all material respects with applicable laws, including, without limitation applicable environmental, health, safety and financial responsibility laws, rules and regulations, applicable to the use of the Vessel for bulk crude oil or finished lubricating products transportation. Owner covenants that it will maintain at all times during the Term of this Agreement insurance coverage for sudden and accidental pollution of $500,000,000.
perturbation.justification: The changed text explicitly states that the Owner is 'not responsible for compliance with any applicable laws and regulations,' which directly contradicts the 'Contradicted Text' stating that 'Owner shall comply in all material respects with applicable laws...'. This creates a direct and undeniable conflict regarding the Owner's obligation to comply with applicable laws, making the contract inconsistent and potentially unenforceable.

perturbation.original_text: Either party hereto shall have the right to terminate this Agreement in the event of a breach by the other party of its obligations hereunder, subject to 10 days prior written notice of such breach given by the non-breaching party to the breaching party and the opportunity for such breaching party to cure such breach during such 10 day period.
perturbation.changed_text: Either party hereto shall have the right to terminate this Agreement in the event of a breach by the other party of its obligations hereunder, subject to 30 days prior written notice of such breach given by the non-breaching party to the breaching party and the opportunity for such breaching party to cure such breach during such 10 day period.
perturbation.explanation: The change increases the prior written notice from 10 days to 30 days. This change introduces an in-text contradiction since the breaching party only has 10 days to cure the breach but has 30 days to be notified of such breach. This creates a contradictory enforcement issue, and uncertainty in how termination is managed.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Either party hereto shall have the right to terminate this Agreement in the event of a breach by the other party of its obligations hereunder, subject to 30 days prior written notice of such breach given by the non-breaching party to the breaching party and the opportunity for such breaching party to cure such breach during such 10 day period. Upon any such termination, this Agreement shall thereafter have no further force or effect except as to already accrued rights and obligations, which shall continue until satisfied.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the notice period from 10 to 30 days, while the cure period remains at 10 days. This creates a direct contradiction with the contradicted text, which states a 30-day notice period but only allows for a 10-day cure period. Since the cure period is shorter than the notification period, it creates an inconsistency in fulfilling obligations, thus creating an in-text contradiction as per the definition: inconsistencies also lead to in-text contradictions when different sections of a contract provide conflicting deadlines, obligations, or penalties.
